steps.instruction: This is a slightly modified version of the Pierce™ BCA Protein Assay (ThermoFisher Scientific). Figure 1: Graphical depiction of procedure summary, from ThermoScientific

Download this Excel spreadsheet and enter the number of unknown samples in cell B6 to calculate your total volume of WR. It will also calculate your required volumes of Reagent A and Reagent B to achieve a 20:1 concentration of Reagent A to Reagent B. BCA Protein Assay Calculation Template.xlsx In this protocol, the assay is performed in triplicates. If more replicates are desired, the modify the spreadsheet so your number of replicates, samples, and unknowns are accurate. This spreadsheet will be used later in this protocol to calculate sample absorbance and perform a linear regression to calculate the concentrations of your unknown samples.
To create a 1:8 ratio of sample:WR ... Add 200 µL of the prepared WR to each well Add 25 µL of each sample in triplicate Pipette 10X per well to mix. Each well has a final volume of 225 µL .
Cover the plate with a semi-permeable seal, and incubate at 37 °C static for 00:30:00 .
With the seal on, cool the plate to Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Remove the seal and dispose in biohazard. Measure the absorbance at 562 nm on the BioTek Synergy H1 microplate reader. Wavelengths from 540–590 nm have been used successfully with this method.

Input the absorbance data into the BCA Protein Assay Calculations Template. Add more unknown rows if needed. The spreadsheet will normalize your data based off the background noise measured in your blanks and perform a linear regression on the absorbance measurements of your unknown samples to find the concentrations. - If the assay was performed in more replicates, add additional columns and modify the formulas as to include your additional absorbance data. Adding a column between existing replicate columns will update your formulas automatically. - If the assay was performed with more unknowns, insert additional rows between the preexisting unknown rows to keep your formulas inclusive of all your data.
Empty benchtop sharps container into larger floor sharps container, and store materials at their designated temperature. Dispose of the 96w assay plate in the biohazard waste bin.

Component Storage Temperature 1 BCA Assay Reagent A 15 - 30 C 2 BCA Assay Reagent B 15 - 30 C 3 BSA Protein Standards 15 - 30 C (4C if previously opened) Table 1: Required reagents and respective storage temperature

In 2 mL tubes, make nine standards in a working range of 25-2,000 µg/mL from the formulas in the following table. These will be used to make a standard curve for linear regression later in the experiment. Vial Volume of Diluent (µL) Volume and Source of BSA (µL) Final BSA Conc. (µg/mL) 1 A 0 300 µL of stock 2,000 2 B 125 375 µL of stock 1,500 3 C 325 325 µL of stock 1,000 4 D 175 175 µL of vial B 750 5 E 325 325 µL of vial C 500 6 F 325 325 µL of vial E 250 7 G 325 325 µL of vial F 125 8 H 400 100 µL of Vial G 25 9 I 400 0 µL (Blank) Table 2: Dilution volumes for standard samples
